13.excerpt: Move that the Town vote to amend the By-Laws by changing in its entirety the table entitled Classification and Pay Plan that is incorporated by reference into Article 24, Section 3, Paragraph 3.10 with the new Classification and Pay Plan as follows: FINANCE COMMITTEE PUBLIC HEARING AND DISCUSSION Mr. Errickson presented this Article and explained that these are minor changes. In Grade 2, there are three job titles that are being modified. In grade 3, there were minor adjustments to better reflect actual duties. Finally, in grade 4, there was an adjustment to the Director of the Council on Aging and Human Services title (essentially reverting to the previous title). There also is a new position for “Substitute Library Assistant” in the part time pay plan (Grade 2). A committee member observed that for some of the technology based positions, the grade and upper range appears over-stated. This is a recurring question. Mr. Errickson indicated that it will be reviewed with the IT Director, but that the chart reflects rather broad ranges and that employees are at various points within the ranges. A member sought information about FTEs/Headcounts over time. That question was beyond the sc
13.floor_impact: Likely article motion text; compare with warrant and FinCom motion before floor action.
13.motion_text: Move that the Town vote to amend the By-Laws by changing in its entirety the table entitled Classification and Pay Plan that is incorporated by reference into Article 24, Section 3, Paragraph 3.10 with the new Classification and Pay Plan as follows: FINANCE COMMITTEE PUBLIC HEARING AND DISCUSSION Mr. Errickson presented this Article and explained that these are minor changes. In Grade 2, there are three job titles that are being modified. In grade 3, there were minor adjustments to better reflect actual duties. Finally, in grade 4, there was an adjustment to the Director of the Council on Aging and Human Services title (essentially reverting to the previous title). There also is a new position for “Substitute Library Assistant” in the part time pay plan (Grade 2). A committee member observed that for some of the technology based positions, the grade and upper range appears over-stated. This is a recurring question. Mr. Errickson indicated that it will be reviewed with the IT Director, but that the chart reflects rather broad ranges and that employees are at various points within the ranges. A member sought information about FTEs/Headcounts over time. That question was beyond the scope of the general Pay Plan, and would be more appropriately answered by an analysis by the Payroll Department, which would be forwarded the question. “Redlines” – Strike-through = Deleted Italic = New/Revised The committee voted 13-0-0 to recommend Favorable Action on the subject matter of Article 13. ~~ END OF ARTICLE ~~

meeting_level_motions.title: Motion: Reading of Articles and Motions

meeting_level_motions.excerpt: TOWN MEETING RESOLUTION HONORING FRANK FOSS WHEREAS, Frank and Charlene Foss moved to Natick in 1978 and raised their two sons Frank Jr. and Jeffrey who both graduated from Natick High School; and WHEREAS, Frank Foss has been a volunteer and participant in local government for thirty-four years beginning when he won a seat on Town Meeting in 1990; and WHEREAS, Frank continued as a Town Meeting member while he served for twelve years on the Finance Committee including as Chair for four years; and WHEREAS, during his tenure on the Finance Committee, Frank served as their representative on many committees including the Municipal Complex Building Committee, the 2 nd Ambulance Study Committee and the Downtown Parking Study Committee; and WHEREAS, in 2007 Frank ran for Moderator; the election was a tie, and he was appointed for one year by the Select Board. He ran again in 2008 and won and continued to serve until March 2025; and WHEREAS, Frank spent many hours working with the administration preparing and presiding over Town Meeting for the next 18 years. He was very instrumental in encouraging and implementing electronic voting; he started the annual new Town Meeting member orientation
meeting_level_motions.motion_text: TOWN MEETING RESOLUTION HONORING FRANK FOSS WHEREAS, Frank and Charlene Foss moved to Natick in 1978 and raised their two sons Frank Jr. and Jeffrey who both graduated from Natick High School; and WHEREAS, Frank Foss has been a volunteer and participant in local government for thirty-four years beginning when he won a seat on Town Meeting in 1990; and WHEREAS, Frank continued as a Town Meeting member while he served for twelve years on the Finance Committee including as Chair for four years; and WHEREAS, during his tenure on the Finance Committee, Frank served as their representative on many committees including the Municipal Complex Building Committee, the 2 nd Ambulance Study Committee and the Downtown Parking Study Committee; and WHEREAS, in 2007 Frank ran for Moderator; the election was a tie, and he was appointed for one year by the Select Board. He ran again in 2008 and won and continued to serve until March 2025; and WHEREAS, Frank spent many hours working with the administration preparing and presiding over Town Meeting for the next 18 years. He was very instrumental in encouraging and implementing electronic voting; he started the annual new Town Meeting member orientation program; hosted a Pegasus show before every session of Town Meeting to inform the citizenry of the issues before the meeting; and he was absolutely critical in setting up the process and procedures that allowed and facilitated on-line Town Meeting during the pandemic. WHEREAS, Frank always tried to honor the processes and purpose of Town Meeting through thoughtful appointments to the Finance and other committees assuring that different voices and opinions were represented; and WHEREAS, Frank’s service to Natick will continue as an elected Town Meeting member and a Constable; Now, therefore be it resolved that Town Meeting hereby honors and thanks Frank Foss for his many years of past and future service to the Town.
open_items: Confirm parsed motion text against the official PDF.
Determine precedence, scope, and vote threshold before floor use.
meeting_level_motions.title: Resolution Honoring Frank Foss

motions.motion_text: TOWN MEETING RESOLUTION HONORING FRANK FOSS WHEREAS, Frank and Charlene Foss moved to Natick in 1978 and raised their two sons Frank Jr. and Jeffrey who both graduated from Natick High School; and WHEREAS, Frank Foss has been a volunteer and participant in local government for thirty-four years beginning when he won a seat on Town Meeting in 1990; and WHEREAS, Frank continued as a Town Meeting member while he served for twelve years on the Finance Committee including as Chair for four years; and WHEREAS, during his tenure on the Finance Committee, Frank served as their representative on many committees including the Municipal Complex Building Committee, the 2 nd Ambulance Study Committee and the Downtown Parking Study Committee; and WHEREAS, in 2007 Frank ran for Moderator; the election was a tie, and he was appointed for one year by the Select Board. He ran again in 2008 and won and continued to serve until March 2025; and WHEREAS, Frank spent many hours working with the administration preparing and presiding over Town Meeting for the next 18 years. He was very instrumental in encouraging and implementing electronic voting; he started the annual new Town Meeting member orientation program; hosted a Pegasus show before every session of Town Meeting to inform the citizenry of the issues before the meeting; and he was absolutely critical in setting up the process and procedures that allowed and facilitated on-line Town Meeting during the pandemic. WHEREAS, Frank always tried to honor the processes and purpose of Town Meeting through thoughtful appointments to the Finance and other committees assuring that different voices and opinions were represented; and WHEREAS, Frank’s service to Natick will continue as an elected Town Meeting member and a Constable; Now, therefore be it resolved that Town Meeting hereby honors and thanks Frank Foss for his many years of past and future service to the Town.

motions.title: Article 11 Motion D Amendment 1 Poliandri

motions.excerpt: Move that the Town vote to amend the By-Laws by changing in its entirety the table entitled Classification and Pay Plan that is incorporated by reference into Article 24, Section 3, Paragraph 3.10 with the new Classification and Pay Plan as follows: FINANCE COMMITTEE PUBLIC HEARING AND DISCUSSION Mr. Errickson presented this Article and explained that these are minor changes. In Grade 2, there are three job titles that are being modified. In grade 3, there were minor adjustments to better reflect actual duties. Finally, in grade 4, there was an adjustment to the Director of the Council on Aging and Human Services title (essentially reverting to the previous title). There also is a new position for “Substitute Library Assistant” in the part time pay plan (Grade 2). A committee member observed that for some of the technology based positions, the grade and upper range appears over-stated. This is a recurring question. Mr. Errickson indicated that it will be reviewed with the IT Director, but that the chart reflects rather broad ranges and that employees are at various points within the ranges. A member sought information about FTEs/Headcounts over time. That question was beyond the sc
motions.floor_impact: Likely article motion text; compare with warrant and FinCom motion before floor action.
motions.motion_text: Move that the Town vote to amend the By-Laws by changing in its entirety the table entitled Classification and Pay Plan that is incorporated by reference into Article 24, Section 3, Paragraph 3.10 with the new Classification and Pay Plan as follows: FINANCE COMMITTEE PUBLIC HEARING AND DISCUSSION Mr. Errickson presented this Article and explained that these are minor changes. In Grade 2, there are three job titles that are being modified. In grade 3, there were minor adjustments to better reflect actual duties. Finally, in grade 4, there was an adjustment to the Director of the Council on Aging and Human Services title (essentially reverting to the previous title). There also is a new position for “Substitute Library Assistant” in the part time pay plan (Grade 2). A committee member observed that for some of the technology based positions, the grade and upper range appears over-stated. This is a recurring question. Mr. Errickson indicated that it will be reviewed with the IT Director, but that the chart reflects rather broad ranges and that employees are at various points within the ranges. A member sought information about FTEs/Headcounts over time. That question was beyond the scope of the general Pay Plan, and would be more appropriately answered by an analysis by the Payroll Department, which would be forwarded the question. “Redlines” – Strike-through = Deleted Italic = New/Revised The committee voted 13-0-0 to recommend Favorable Action on the subject matter of Article 13. ~~ END OF ARTICLE ~~
